title: Real-time Reverse Transcription Polymerase Chain Reaction (RT-qPCR)
author: Izabela M Rezende
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.pw8dphw

steps.instruction: Each sample is tested at least in duplicate, or triplicate.
The final reaction volume in this protocol is 20µl.
Estimate the appropriate amount of each reagent for each test/sample.
Determine the number of reactions to be set up, including negative control from the RNA extraction, for the non-template control and for the positive control reactions.
Add 1 or 2 reactions to this number to compensate for pipetting error.

Prepare the reaction mix (minus the RNA template) by combining the GoTaq® Probe qPCR Master Mix with dUTP, GoScript™ RT Mix for 1-Step RT-qPCR, primers, hydrolysis probe and Nuclease-Free Water as described below. Vortex briefly to mix. %s %s *The GoTaq® Probe qPCR Master Mix included in the GoTaq® Probe 1-Step RT-qPCR System is formulated with dUTP. When dUTP is incorporated into the amplification products, the amplicons are susceptible to degradation by uracilDNA glycosylase (UNG); this allows you to incorporate UNG into subsequent reactions to control possible carryover contamination.
Add the appropriate volume of reaction mix (without the RNA template) to each PCR tube or well of an optical grade PCR plate.
Add the RNA template (or water for the no-template control reactions) to the appropriate wells of the reaction plate.

Seal the tubes or optical plate; centrifuge briefly to collect the contents of the wells at the bottom. Protect from extended light exposure or elevated temperatures before cycling. The samples are ready for thermal cycling.
When the cycling ends, analyze the results according to the manufacturer's instructions.
RNA extraction control and non-template controls must be negative and positive control must be positive.
To be considered positive a sample must present at least two replicates with amplification equal of bellow the cycle threshold of 37.
